Opportunities.name: Great Lakes Orthopedic Center (GLOC)
Opportunities.Size: 4 locs (2 pilot) · ~2K calls/wk · Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale
Opportunities.why_exciting: Solv's first Monarch design partner — signed Apr 27. Athena ortho reference actively being built.
Key notes: Signed Apr 27; kickoff doc live May 1 — Carli collecting Athena API credentials + requesting preview instance copy-down; Kennedy delivering Maya DID phone requirements to GLOC team
Jim Stilley (CEO, former Navy Commander) and Deanna Dean (Clinical Manager, 10 yrs) are implementation owners on GLOC side
Jim's leadership team on vacation until ~May 16 — formal kickoff call targeted after that date
Kapeesh requested a Google Drive SOP to document all design partner onboarding learnings as a repeatable template
Pilot is free — deal becomes margin-positive once resolution rate hits ~35%; rate unproven in ortho context
next_steps: GLOC to complete Athena 3rd-party auth form (PRD + preview instance) — Owner: Carli — Due: ASAP
GLOC to confirm DID phone line exists or provision one (24–96hr lead time) — Owner: Kennedy — Due: ASAP
Brantley/Kapeesh to build Monarch onboarding SOP in Google Drive — Due: This week

Opportunities.name: CLS Health
Opportunities.Size: $3,500/mo · up to 3K telemed visits · +1% SolvPay +$0.50 ClearPay · 250+ providers · Texas
Opportunities.why_exciting: President wants Solv to replace Luma for ALL primary care scheduling — scope could expand far beyond telehealth origin.
Key notes: Full end-to-end telehealth demo delivered May 5 — Dr. Baba (decision-maker) is engaged but explicitly gating on ECW proof: 'if this doesn't integrate with ECW, let us know'
HMO/assigned-PCP billing logic unresolved — workaround (place of service 20 or cash-pay at $60–75/visit) needs validation before launch
CLS pricing counter open: per-encounter hybrid model requested, 3–6 months free ramp, 6-month initial term (vs. 1-year), waive $2K impl fee
Danny Cavic asked May 6 to compile all CLS feedback/questions into a doc for Maria Kwok (newly added to channel) to review and add details

Two-part deal: UC telehealth now + specialties as design partner separately; Kapeesh call on specialty scope (referral/auth workflow) still unscheduled; Luma disqualified by CLS on dynamic eligibility
next_steps: Danny to compile all CLS feedback/questions into doc for Maria Kwok review — Owner: Danny — Due: ASAP
Deliver ECW integration proof video (HIPAA-blurred) + ECW reference customer (multi-provider, Texas preferred) — Owner: Brantley/Danny — Due: Urgent
Schedule Kapeesh call on specialty design partner scope (ClearPay referral/auth workflow) — Owner: Kapeesh — Due: TBD

Opportunities.name: Premier Ortho

Key notes: Formally paused since Mar 11 — Assort Health exclusivity clause blocks all movement until their exit resolves; no timeline given
Jon proactively reached out 3/11 rather than ghosting, confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear
Deal was at near-signature in late Feb (DocuSign pending) before Assort surfaced — Jon knows the product well
Assort's ToS prevents even a listening phase while still under contract
Last active check-in Apr 7 (no update); every-2-week passive cadence means a check-in is now overdue
next_steps: Overdue check-in with Jon on Assort exit status — Owner: Brantley — Due: THIS WEEK
Re-qualify urgency and deal state once Assort exit resolves — deal has been paused 8+ weeks

Opportunities.name: OS1 Orthopedic & Sports Injury

Key notes: Note: Based on #prosp-monarch-deals only — no dedicated Slack channel or Granola data found

Randy (board member) scheduled to reconvene week of Apr 28 after internal board meeting — May 1–4 reconnect window has now passed with no visible update
Pitch focus: ClearPay + Maya to improve cash flow and reduce headcount pressure without large upfront cost
Randy is board-level not operational — no day-to-day champion identified yet

Not a Monarch ICP deal; redirected to standard Solv sales motion

next_steps: No action — closed. Revisit only if Osvaldo re-engages post-Athena go-live

Opportunities.name: Total Primary Care
Opportunities.why_exciting: 47 locations with real acquisition pain — not viable on current timeline; strategic revisit Q4 2026 at earliest.
Key notes: Cut off Apr 7 — Rikin (CoS) won't replace the CTO-built scheduler and is 6–9 months from any real Monarch commitment
Only viable path was acquisition-layer model alongside their existing scheduler — non-standard deal with unclear Solv appetite
Rikin is actively piloting a competing voice agent and was skeptical of Maya
next_steps: No near-term action. Revisit Q4 2026 if acquisition-layer deal model gains internal Solv traction — Owner: Teresa/Kapeesh
